mapping.a_element: differential entropy of the density, being maximized over a class of densities
mapping.b_element: channel capacity = max mutual information I(X;Y), which for an AWGN channel reduces to maximizing the output differential entropy h(Y)

mapping.a_element: moment constraints (all densities share the log-normal's moments)
mapping.b_element: the input cost constraints: average power (second moment) and kurtosis (fourth moment)

mapping.a_element: the class of admissible densities satisfying the moment constraints
mapping.b_element: the class of admissible input distributions satisfying the power and kurtosis constraints

mapping.a_element: the entropy-extremal density (the counterexample density achieving larger entropy)
mapping.b_element: the capacity-achieving input distribution (discrete amplitude, concentric multi-ring geometry)

mapping.a_element: Lagrangian / variational characterization of the moment-constrained extremizer
mapping.b_element: KKT / variational characterization of the optimal moment-constrained input

brief_justification: Both are instances of the same maximum-entropy-under-moment-constraints machinery solved by Lagrangian/variational methods: A asks which density maximizes differential entropy given fixed moments, while B's AWGN capacity under power (2nd-moment) and kurtosis (4th-moment) constraints reduces to maximizing output entropy under input moment constraints, with the moment constraints and the extremal-distribution characterization playing identical roles; the chief divergence is that B's added noise channel drives its optimum to a discrete multi-ring support rather than a continuous density.
